msg: Dedup judge unusable on this host even with --judge-provider claude (claude CLI exits 1: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY takes precedence over claude.ai login inside the judge subprocess). Ran manual title-token dup scan instead: WI-1154 is NOT a dup of open work — it is a recurrence of CLOSED WI-558 (same eval scenario SL-DU02, Fixed In 223f636d). Triaged to Backlog as Bug/P1 (recurrence-after-fix, prior fix provably insufficient); root-cause researcher dispatched. Cross-lane note: other shepherds' --dedup calls will hit the same judge crash.

ACK received; WI-1284 noted (won't re-file dedup-judge blocker; keeping manual title-token scan). Applying regression-diagnosis directive: WI-1154 researcher supplemented to (a) find whether WI-558's fix shipped a break test, (b) explain why it passed while the live leak recurred, (c) classify the regression cause (prompt drift / model-or-router change / over-narrow fix / leak-always-existed-now-tested via WI-560/WI-1148 envelope-flow coverage), (d) specify what the new red-green break test must pin (exact SL-DU02 step-by-step extraction, correct flow+rung+tier, --live Tier-2 if needed). Regression root cause will be reported as a follow-up outbox decision once the researcher returns.

REGRESSION ROOT CAUSE (as requested). WI-558 shipped NO deterministic break test — commit 223f636d was +4/-1 to one prompt file (exchange-prompts.ts) plus a Tier-1 snapshot regen; commit msg states 'Live Tier-2 keys unavailable... Tier-1 snapshot diff is the verification artifact'. Tier-1 only proves the rule TEXT is present, never that the model OBEYS it. Classification: over-narrow prompt-only fix, never behaviorally (live) tested. The leak almost certainly always existed for weak minor-routed models (13yo->under-18 fallback->gpt-4o-mini, router.ts:625-631,679-681); it only SURFACED once WI-560 made live envelope-flow evals actually invoke the model on SL-DU02 (safety-probes.emitsEnvelope=true). Net: a prompt-only control cannot be defended by a deterministic test, so its efficacy is model-dependent and regressed silently. The 'which WI enabled live' has WI-560 confirmed in code; WI-1148 unverifiable by grep.

FIX SCOPE decided within mandate. WI-1154 fix = Option B (server-side procedure-leak reply gate, post-parseEnvelope, mirroring the existing applySourceAuditSafetyFallback seam at session-exchange.ts:3724-3736; fail-closed to a decline-but-keep-frame reply) + a co-located DETERMINISTIC red-green break test feeding the exact SL-DU02 opium->heroin 4-step extraction + an over-block guard test (legit SL-DU01 what/why NOT blocked) + staged --live safety-probes eval evidence. This is the load-bearing, testable, model-independent fix and satisfies the AGENTS.md 'prompt signals need a server-side hard cap' rule. DEFERRING researcher's Option C (under-18 gpt-4o-mini routing floor): it needs net-new llmRoutingRung plumbing (AGENTS.md:342 'not yet in source'), is still prompt-dependent (not deterministically testable), and lifting every minor off the cheap tier is a COST/product call. Flagging Option C to operator as optional defense-in-depth follow-up, NOT gating the P1 fix on it. Escalating that one narrow point below.

WI-781 DECIDED within mandate: FLIP CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED ON (not defer). Researcher verified via LIVE read-only SQL (doppler -c stg/prd, pg client) that the WI's deferral condition is already satisfied: concepts/concept_mastery FK already repoints to person(id) on BOTH stg+prd (no profiles table exists), and RLS isolation policies are live (WI-1104 Closed/Done, migration 0125). Not a pure no-code close — resolves to a SMALL BUILD: flag flip (concept-capture.ts:19) + correct schema-code drift (concept-mastery.ts still .references(profiles.id) vs live person.id) + drizzle-kit generate matching additive migration (must verify no-op/idempotent vs live) + update parked-guard test + architecture.md:139 canon line (lockstep) + positive write-path test. NOTE: CHALLENGE_ROUND_RUNTIME_ENABLED=false in stg+prd (Doppler-confirmed) so the flip is INERT in those envs until Challenge Round is separately enabled — flip is low-risk/pre-launch. Dispatching small builder in parallel with the WI-1154 P1 (separate worktrees; both touch session-exchange.ts in different regions ~974 vs ~3724, merge-order rebase expected).

MECHANISM CHECK answered: CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED is a CODE DEFAULT, not a Doppler/prod-env var. Verified by grep: 'export const CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED = false;' at apps/api/src/services/concept-capture.ts:19 (module-level constant, imported by session-exchange.ts:974 + tests); no process.env / config-object / Doppler read anywhere. Condition (1)=code-default -> proceeding through the normal PR->review->merge pipeline within mandate (NOT a prod-config flip; no operator gate needed). Condition (2): instructed the WI-781 builder to embed the live-SQL evidence (FK->person(id) on stg+prd, no profiles table, RLS policies live, WI-1104 Closed/0125, concepts/concept_mastery 0/0 rows) in the PR body so the reviewer/operator can independently verify the deferral gate. Builder already dispatched and running.

WI-1155 fix scope decided within mandate (engineering approach, no product fork). Two clusters. Cluster A (insufficient=false): (A1 SGA04) SERVER-SIDE derivation — the audit already strips unsupported source-bound phrases at exchanges.ts:1276-1293 but forgets to set insufficient=true; add that + a red-green unit test. Clean deterministic win. (A2 HW04/HW02) PROMPT hardening (explicitly-incomplete/blurry source -> force insufficient=true + ask for missing/clearer source); the eval's photo-invention regex is scenario-brittle so a server truncation-detector is DEFERRED (only if prompt-only fails the --live re-run -> report back, I capture a follow-up WI then; not bundling into this P2). Cluster B (P17 teach_back_assessment absent): (B1) PROMPT hardening — rubric mandatory every teach-back turn, FIRST; (B2) server judge fallback (port runChallengeRoundGrader as runTeachBackGrader) held in RESERVE, only if B1 fails --live. BUILD SEQUENCING: WI-1155's A1 edits applySourceAuditSafetyFallback (exchanges.ts), the same seam WI-1154 wraps at session-exchange.ts:3724, plus shared probe prompts/snapshots -> WI-1155 build dispatches AFTER WI-1154 merges. Refining WI-1155 to Ready now; holding the build.

APPROVED a builder pre-destructive pause: WI-781's schema migration (new 0129_damp_ego.sql) is intentionally HAND-EDITED from drizzle-kit's raw output to be idempotent across a dev-vs-stg/prd FK-name split. drizzle raw would DROP CONSTRAINT '..._profiles_id_fk' unconditionally -> aborts drizzle-kit migrate on stg/prd (where the FK is already '..._person_id_fk' from the out-of-band profiles->person cutover). Rewrote with DO-block IF EXISTS/IF NOT EXISTS guards -> no-op on stg/prd, real repoint on dev. VERIFIED before approving: 0124_striped_thing.sql header documents the IDENTICAL out-of-band-profiles-drop situation for retrieval_events (operator-authorized precedent); 0129 is a NEW migration (after 0128) so immutability-guard-safe, no allowlist entry; concepts/concept_mastery are 0/0 rows on all envs (empty-table repoint, low blast radius); Rollback note present. Builder told to flag the intentional hand-edit in the PR body so the reviewer doesn't read it as drift.

HOLD-FOR-ORCHESTRATOR-REVIEW acknowledged; WI-781 builder HALTED before PR-finalize/complete/merge until your go. Four artifacts: (1) SCOPE — agree it is arguably separable. The flag is a code-default one-liner; the FK-repoint migration exists only to correct PRE-EXISTING schema-code drift: packages/database/src/schema/concept-mastery.ts has declared .references(profiles.id) since these tables were created — the SAME drift class as subjects.ts:54 (tracked as WI-1128), and independent of the flag. Runtime does not need it (Drizzle INSERTs emit no FK clause; live stg/prd FK already = person, enforced by DB). The ONLY thing that could couple them is the positive write-path TEST (AC: concept_mastery rows land) IF it runs against a DB whose FK still names _profiles_id_fk. I have queried the builder for exactly that one fact (which DB the write-path test hits + separable yes/no). PROPOSAL: if separable, SPLIT — WI-781 ships flag+guard-test-update+integration-comment+architecture.md:139 canon (code-only, normal pipeline); the concept-mastery.ts FK repoint + migration 0129 become a NEW schema-hygiene WI (sibling to WI-1128) that carries your in-seat schema review + operator deploy gate. If the test structurally needs the dev repoint, they stay coupled under your review. Awaiting builder's answer before I finalize the split. (2) IDEMPOTENCY PROOF — file: apps/api/drizzle/0129_damp_ego.sql (in worktree .worktrees/WI-781/). Four DO-blocks. DROP side: `DO $$ BEGIN IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='concept_mastery_profile_id_profiles_id_fk') THEN ALTER TABLE concept_mastery DROP CONSTRAINT ...; END IF; END $$;` (same for concepts). ADD side: `DO $$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='concept_mastery_profile_id_person_id_fk') THEN ALTER TABLE concept_mastery ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY (profile_id) REFERENCES person(id) ON DELETE cascade; END IF; END $$;` (same for concepts). Note your point is correct — Postgres has NO ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS, hence the DO-block pg_constraint check. EVIDENCE of behavior: builder live-verified via pg_constraint on BOTH envs this session — stg/prd already have _person_id_fk (ADD's IF NOT EXISTS skips) and DO NOT have _profiles_id_fk (DROP's IF EXISTS skips) => BOTH branches no-op on stg/prd. dev has _profiles_id_fk (DROP fires) and lacks _person_id_fk (ADD fires) => real repoint on dev. I did NOT run drizzle-kit migrate against stg/prd (deploy-gated, not mine to run); the guard logic + the live constraint-name snapshot IS the no-op proof. If you want a literal dry-run, I can have the builder run drizzle-kit migrate against a dev clone (transform case); the stg/prd no-op is proven by the constraint-name state, not runnable without touching prod. (3) ROLLBACK — present in the 0129 header: tables are 0/0 rows on every env (empty-table FK repoint, no data at risk); revert = swap guarded DROP/ADD pairs (drop _person_id_fk, re-add _profiles_id_fk REFERENCES profiles.id), only meaningful where profiles still exists (i.e. dev). (4) The file path is above; it is uncommitted-or-local in the worktree, unmerged. Precedent I verified before approving: 0124_striped_thing.sql header documents the IDENTICAL out-of-band profiles-drop + FK-repoint for retrieval_events (operator-authorized). 0129 is a NEW migration (after 0128) so immutability-safe, no allowlist entry. Prod application stays on the operator deploy gate regardless. Standing by for the builder's separability fact, then I finalize scope and hand you the migration to read before any merge.

RESOLVED (ref se-inbox-006 scope): SPLIT DONE. Builder confirmed clean separability — the write-path integration test seeds BOTH a profiles and a person row sharing an id, so it passes against either FK target; the flag flip is not structurally coupled to the migration. Actions taken within mandate: (1) WI-781 reduced to the code-only flag flip (CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED=true + guard-test update + write-path test + profile-isolation comment + architecture.md canon) — proceeding on the normal PR->review->merge pipeline per your pre-authorization ('flag-flip may proceed independently IF separable'). HALT lifted for WI-781; builder told to first run the affected api integration suite with the flag ON to prove no sibling seeder breaks now the captureConceptMastery write path is active against the current FK->profiles CI DB. (2) The schema-hygiene half is now WI-1288 (Hygiene, P2, related WI-781/WI-1128/WI-1104): the concept-mastery.ts FK repoint (profiles.id->person.id) + the idempotent migration 0129 (FULL SQL preserved in WI-1288's AC) + the person-row seeder sweep across concept-capture-touching integration tests (needed once CI's FK flips to person). WI-1288 is PARKED at Captured pending YOUR in-seat schema review of 0129 before it merges + the operator deploy gate for prod. It inherited WS-31 workstream from the --origin-wi; re-home it to a schema-hygiene workstream if you prefer. NEEDS-ORCHESTRATOR: your read/go on WI-1288's 0129 before I refine+dispatch its builder. Meta: capture's dedup judge also crashes on this host (codex CLI arg mismatch this time; claude CLI the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY issue earlier) — WI-1284 territory; capture created the row anyway, structured recall found no dups.
